inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior lost — violates .review-rules/copy.md — The original `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (follows links) and `fs.copyFileSync` (follows links), so symlinks-to-directories were recursed into and their content materialized. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, copying symlinks as-is. This causes dangling or differently-resolved symlinks in the output when `publicDir` or a create-vite template contains symlinks. The project rule explicitly flags this substitution.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior lost in create-vite template copy — Same symlink-dereference regression as in prepareOutDir: `fs.cpSync` with default `dereference: false` will copy symlinks as symlinks instead of materializing their target content, breaking template scaffolding that relies on `copyDir`'s follow-symlink behavior.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Recursive-copy guard removed — outDir nested in publicDir now throws — The original `copyDir` in utils.ts contained a guard that skipped the destination directory when `outDir` was nested inside `publicDir` (e.g. `publicDir: 'public'` + `build.outDir: 'public/dist'`). `fs.cpSync` does not have this guard and throws `ERR_FS_CP_RECURSIVE` in this configuration. Observable behavior changes from 'copy everything except nested output' to 'throw and abort the build'.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Unannounced behavioral change: COPYFILE_FICLONE mode added — Both call sites add `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE`, requesting copy-on-write reflinks on supported filesystems. The original `copyDir` used `fs.copyFileSync` with default mode `0` (plain copy). While COPYFILE_FICLONE falls back gracefully, it is an unannounced behavioral change beyond the stated intent of the refactor.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Unannounced behavioral change: COPYFILE_FICLONE mode added in create-vite — Same COPYFILE_FICLONE behavioral change as in prepareOutDir — the original `copyDir` used plain copies, but the replacement adds reflink mode.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

This PR directly violates the `.review-rules/copy.md` rule by removing `copyDir` and replacing it with `fs.cpSync`, losing symlink-dereference behavior and a recursive-copy guard that the scaffolding and public-dir copy depend on. Two errors (symlink regressions), two warnings (guard removal and export breakage), and two info-level behavioral changes (COPYFILE_FICLONE addition) are identified. The recommendation is to not merge as-is and instead improve `copyDir` internally while preserving its contract.

**6** finding(s): 🔴 2  ·  🟡 2  ·  🔵 2

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior lost — violates .review-rules/copy.md — The original `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (follows links) and `fs.copyFileSync` (follows links), so symlinks-to-directories were recursed into and their content materialized. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, copying symlinks as-is. This causes dangling or differently-resolved symlinks in the output when `publicDir` or a create-vite template contains symlinks. The project rule explicitly flags this substitution.
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior lost in create-vite template copy — Same symlink-dereference regression as in prepareOutDir: `fs.cpSync` with default `dereference: false` will copy symlinks as symlinks instead of materializing their target content, breaking template scaffolding that relies on `copyDir`'s follow-symlink behavior.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Recursive-copy guard removed — outDir nested in publicDir now throws — The original `copyDir` in utils.ts contained a guard that skipped the destination directory when `outDir` was nested inside `publicDir` (e.g. `publicDir: 'public'` + `build.outDir: 'public/dist'`). `fs.cpSync` does not have this guard and throws `ERR_FS_CP_RECURSIVE` in this configuration. Observable behavior changes from 'copy everything except nested output' to 'throw and abort the build'.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Removing copyDir export from shared utils.ts may break other consumers — The `copyDir` export is deleted entirely from utils.ts, which is a shared module. The diff only shows `prepareOutDir.ts` updating its import, but any other file in the codebase or downstream consumers importing `copyDir` will break at import time.
- 🔵 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:91` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Unannounced behavioral change: COPYFILE_FICLONE mode added — Both call sites add `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE`, requesting copy-on-write reflinks on supported filesystems. The original `copyDir` used `fs.copyFileSync` with default mode `0` (plain copy). While COPYFILE_FICLONE falls back gracefully, it is an unannounced behavioral change beyond the stated intent of the refactor.
- 🔵 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:649` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Unannounced behavioral change: COPYFILE_FICLONE mode added in create-vite — Same COPYFILE_FICLONE behavioral change as in prepareOutDir — the original `copyDir` used plain copies, but the replacement adds reflink mode.

---
_Reviewed by: Correctness, Security · glm-5.2 · findings reflect the enabled lenses only_

unplaceable.message: Removing copyDir export from shared utils.ts may break other consumers — The `copyDir` export is deleted entirely from utils.ts, which is a shared module. The diff only shows `prepareOutDir.ts` updating its import, but any other file in the codebase or downstream consumers importing `copyDir` will break at import time.
